locator: Proclamation to the German Nation, 1 February 1933; in Baynes, Norman H., ed., The Speeches of Adolf Hitler, April 1922–August 1939, Vol. 1 (London: Oxford University Press, 1942), p. 156
text: Towering above all class and rank distinctions, it will bring back to our people the consciousness of their racial and political unity and of the obligations this entails.

locator: Program of the NSDAP (25 Points), Point 10, announced 24 February 1920; in Stackelberg, Roderick, and Sally A. Winkle, eds., The Nazi Germany Sourcebook (London: Routledge, 2002), p. 29
text: The activity of the individual must not clash with the interests of the community, but must proceed within its framework and be for the general good.

locator: Art. 1(1), Basic Law for the Federal Republic of Germany, 23 May 1949; official English translation, Deutscher Bundestag, 2012 ed.
text: Human dignity shall be inviolable. To respect and protect it shall be the duty of all state authority.

locator: Art. 79(3), Basic Law for the Federal Republic of Germany, 23 May 1949; official English translation, Deutscher Bundestag, 2012 ed.
text: Amendments to this Basic Law affecting the division of the Federation into Länder, their participation on principle in the legislative process, or the principles laid down in Articles 1 and 20 shall be inadmissible.
